sections.paragraph:
to search for [“LXX. σκάλλω, Psa 76:7. Ch. and Sam. חֲפַס, id. Kindred perhaps to חָפַר, the ר and ש being interchanged”]. Always in Kal metaph. to seek out, e.g. wisdom. Pro 2:4 comp. Pro 20:7. Psa 64:7, יַחְפְּשׂוּ עֹולֹות “they devise wicked things.” (In Chald. and Sam. the proper sense is that of digging the ground, searching in the earth, comp. חָפַר No. I. 2.)

Niphal pass. to be sought out, Oba 1:6.

Piel, to search, Gen 31:35 44:12 followed by an acc. 1Sa 23:23 to search through, 1Ki 20:6 Zep 1:12. Metaph. once Psa 77:7, וַיְחַפֵּשׂ רוּחִי “and my spirit made diligent search.”

Pual-

(1) to be sought, hence to let one’s self be sought for; to hide one’s self, Pro 28:12 compare verse 28 Pro 28:28and Hithpael.

(2) to be devised, Psa 64:7.

Hithpael, properly to allow one’s self to be sought for; to hide one’s self (see Pual No. 1), hence to feign one’s self to be another, to disguise one’s self. 1Sa 28:8. 1Ki 20:38, וַיִּתְחַפֵּשׂ בָּֽאֲפֵר עַל־עֵינָיו “and he disguised himself, having a bandage over his eyes.” 1Ki 22:30. Job 30:18, בְּרָב־כֹּחַ יִתְחַפֵּשׂ לְבוּשִׁי “by (its) great power my garment (i.e. skin) is changed,” comp. verse 19 Job 30:19.
